metadata.title: Reflection Paper on Immune Tolerance Induction in Haemophilia A Patients with Inhibitors

rules.rule_text: Clinical trials in ITI are not required for marketing authorisation of FVIII products. However, management of inhibitor patients and ITI experience with a specific product may be reflected in the SmPC.
rules.verbatim_quote: Clear cut guidance on clinical trials to be performed to endorse an indication claim for ITI cannot be given at present due to the complexity of unresolved scientific questions, the challenging nature of the management of inhibitor patients, the rarity of the condition, and the difficulty to undertake controlled trials.

rules.rule_text: ITI experience with a specific FVIII product may be included in SmPC section 5.1 (Pharmacodynamic properties) with the following standard statement: 'Data on Immune Tolerance Induction (ITI) have been collected in patients with haemophilia A who have developed inhibitors to FVIII.'
rules.verbatim_quote: It is now proposed that ITI experience with a specific product may be included in section 5.1 of its SmPC with the following statement: "Data on Immune Tolerance Induction (ITI) have been collected in patients with haemophilia A who have developed inhibitors to FVIII."

rules.rule_text: Success rates from ITI data should not be included in the SmPC unless the information is robust, as such information may not be meaningful given the many variables that influence outcomes.
rules.verbatim_quote: Success rates from this data, or information on how immune tolerance was induced should not be included unless this information is robust as such information may not be meaningful in view of the many variables that can influence the observed rate.
rules.exceptions: Unless information is robust enough to be meaningful.

rules.rule_text: There is no ITI experience with long-acting modified FVIII products. Regulatory decision on reflection of ITI in product information for these products must be made on a case-by-case basis.
rules.verbatim_quote: However, there is no ITI experience with the long-acting modified products. Regulatory decision on reflection of ITI in the product information is done on a case by case basis.

rules.rule_text: ITI is an individually tailored therapy. Commonly agreed eligibility criteria, treatment regimens regarding dosage and duration, and success criteria for reproducible documentation of efficacy have not been developed.
rules.verbatim_quote: Successful eradication of inhibitors in haemophilia patients through ITI remains an individually tailored therapy which has been subject to reports and discussion over the last 30 years. ... commonly agreed eligibility criteria for concerned patients, treatment regimens regarding dosage and duration as well as success criteria for reproducible documentation of efficacy have not been developed so far.

rules.rule_text: Clinical research to investigate key ITI clinical questions is encouraged and can be supported by European Scientific Advice.
rules.verbatim_quote: Clinical research to investigate the key clinical questions is encouraged and can be supported by European Scientific Advice.
